title: Capture equivalent Breenix CPU0 traces
description: Capture equivalent Breenix CPU0 traces for the same scenarios as the Linux reference set, using comparable instrumentation and preserving exact commands and artifacts.
notes: Reproducible Breenix Parallels CPU0 bundle captured at logs/breenix-parallels-cpu0/20260402-1454-live via scripts/parallels/collect-breenix-cpu0-traces.sh. Bundle shows EL0 entry, queue_empty stuck_tid warnings, PC_ALIGN at ELR=0xffff000040186801 from EL1, soft lockup, and continuing CPU0 timer progress afterward.

comments.author: Ryan Breen
comments.text: Current best hypothesis: blocked-in-syscall or kernel-mode ret-dispatch is resuming a thread with odd elr_el1 (0xffff000040186801, resolves inside ahci::handle_interrupt + 1). This is now the first concrete divergence candidate to falsify before any architectural fix.

title: Isolate first semantic divergence
description: Compare Linux and Breenix traces to isolate the first semantic divergence in CPU 0 timer, IPI, idle-exit, EL0 dispatch, or scheduling behavior. Hypotheses must be falsifiable and recorded with predicted observables.

title: Implement minimal architectural fix
description: Implement the minimal architectural fix for the first confirmed divergence. Remove workaround assumptions instead of layering new ones. The fix must be validated against the same canonical traces.

title: Capture canonical Linux CPU0 traces
description: Capture and preserve canonical Linux CPU0 traces on Parallels/HVF for timer, IPI, idle-exit, and scheduling paths. Treat these as the reference dataset for all later comparisons.
notes: Canonical Linux CPU0 trace bundle captured and verified in logs/linux-probe-cpu0/latest-smoke and logs/linux-probe-cpu0/20260402-073933. Linux on Parallels/HVF shows normal CPU0 arch_timer, IPI, idle-exit, and scheduling behavior.

title: ARM64 CPU0 interrupt-driven SMP investigation
description: Use Linux on Parallels/HVF as the reference model for CPU 0 interrupt-driven SMP semantics. No workaround-mode closures; every code change must be driven by an observed Linux-vs-Breenix divergence and closed only when CPU 0 sustains normal timer, IPI, idle-exit, EL0, and scheduling behavior without special routing.

title: Remove workarounds and add regression verification
description: Remove CPU 0 workaround code once the real fix is proven, then add repeatable verification so the project cannot regress back into workaround mode unnoticed.

title: Factory F1: extract Linux v6.8 GIC-v3 IRQ blueprint
description: Produce a cite-backed prose blueprint of Linux ARM64 v6.8 GIC-v3 IRQ handling from EL1 IRQ entry through EOIR and return, using local or probe-host source and the existing linux-probe evidence. Deliver the blueprint, scratchpad, and exit documentation in a repo-local factory run directory without modifying the Breenix kernel tree.
